content.text:
The test fails exactly as predicted. There was no compilation error (the function and all types exist), and the runtime error is precisely what I predicted: `expected [Function] to throw an error`. The function returns normally instead of throwing because the implementation does not validate that the number of damage entries per item type does not exceed the number of insured items of that type.

## Step 7: Report Completion

```
Red Phase Complete:
**Test Activated**: "should reject claim when damages contain more entries of a type than insured -- two sword damages but only one sword insured"
**Compilation Prediction**: No compilation error expected (function already exists) -- Correct
**Runtime Prediction**: Expected "expected [Function] to throw an error" because claimOffice processes both damages without validating count against insured items -- Correct
**Result**: Test fails as expected with assertion error: "expected [Function] to throw an error"

Proceeding to Green phase.
```

The activated test is in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-4/src/claim-office.spec.ts` at line 823-848. The implementation file is `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-4/src/claim-office.ts`. The test expects `claimOffice` to throw when two sword damages are submitted against a policy that only insures one sword, but the current implementation happily processes both damages and returns a payout result.
